facade_colour: warm red-orange brick, with some variation in colour across the row
roof_type: Gambrel/mansard style with dormers, asphalt shingles

photo_observations.facade_colour_observed: warm red-orange brick, with some variation in colour across the row
photo_observations.facade_condition_notes: Row of 2.5-storey houses. Various conditions - some well maintained, others showing wear. Multiple units visible in this row. Front yards with low fences, bins, and plantings.
photo_observations.window_details: Mix of original and replacement windows. Upper dormers have smaller windows. Some units have white-painted window frames.
photo_observations.door_details: Each unit has its own entrance. Raised entries with steps up from street level.
photo_observations.cornice_details: Simple wood cornice/trim at eave line
photo_observations.porch_details: Second-floor open balconies/porches with white metal railings on some units. Ground level raised entries with steps.
photo_observations.decorative_details: Peaked/triangular dormer gables on upper storey, some with decorative shingle treatment. White metal balcony railings.
photo_observations.roof_type_observed: Gambrel/mansard style with dormers, asphalt shingles
photo_observations.alterations_visible: Some units have vinyl replacement windows. Balconies appear to have been added or rebuilt. Mixed fence styles at front.
photo_observations.notes: Row of Edwardian-era houses with gambrel roofs and dormers. Multiple units visible in the photo. Second-floor balconies with white metal railings on some units. Mature trees partially obscure view.

notes: Row of Edwardian-era houses with gambrel roofs and dormers. Multiple units visible in the photo. Second-floor balconies with white metal railings on some units. Mature trees partially obscure view.

facade_detail.composition: The street elevation spans roughly 5 metres across 2.5 storeys and is capped by a gambrel/mansard style with dormers, asphalt shingles, consistent with its house-form, row typology.
facade_detail.opening_rhythm: The facade keeps a domestic opening rhythm of 2, 2, 1 window bays from lower to upper levels, using double hung, and 1 principal entrance.

facade_description: 144 (cottage), Oxford St area row houses presents a row-house facade within the Kensington Market HCD. The street elevation spans roughly 5 metres across 2.5 storeys and is capped by a gambrel/mansard style with dormers, asphalt shingles, consistent with its house-form, row typology. The facade reads primarily as brick, laid in running bond with light grey mortar joints. The facade keeps a domestic opening rhythm of 2, 2, 1 window bays from lower to upper levels, using double hung, and 1 principal entrance. Character-defining facade elements include cornice.
